title: Biomarkers of the integrated stress response and uses thereof
abstract: The present disclosure provides, based on GDF15 expression, compositions and methods useful in modulating integrated stress response (ISR) and associated diseases or disorders and methods of identifying ISR modulators. The present disclosure also provides methods of treating neurodegenerative conditions and predicting beneficial effects and identifying suitable targets for treatment. Exemplified are elF2B modulators as therapeutic agents.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of treating a neurodegenerative condition, the method comprising: determining the GDF15 expression level in a sample obtained from a patient; comparing the GDF15 expression level in the sample to a reference; determining that the GDF15 expression level in the sample is elevated as compared to the reference; and administering an integrated stress response (ISR) modulator. 2. A method of treating a neurodegenerative condition in a patient comprising administering to the patient an integrated stress response (ISR) modulator, wherein GDF15 expression level is elevated in a sample obtained from the patient prior to administration of the ISR modulator as compared to a reference. 3. A method of treating a patient having integrated stress response dysregulation, the method comprising administering an integrated stress response (ISR) modulator to the patient, wherein GDF15 expression level in a sample obtained from the patient is elevated as compared to a reference. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the reference is determined by measuring the GDF15 expression level in a sample obtained from an aged match healthy subject. 5. A method of evaluating a compound to determine whether the compound can modulate the integrated stress response, the method comprising: administering the compound to a subject; measuring GDF15 expression level in a sample obtained from the subject; and comparing the GDF15 expression level to a reference, wherein a significant difference between the GDF15 expression level and the reference is indicative that the compound can modulate the integrated stress response. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the reference is determined by measuring the GDF15 expression level in a sample obtained from the subject before administering the compound. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the sample is a fluid sample. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the fluid sample is a cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) sample. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the fluid sample is a blood sample. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the blood sample is enriched for central nervous system (CNS)-derived extracellular vesicles (e.g., exosomes). 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the CNS-derived extracellular vesicles are astrocyte- derived exosomes (ADEs). 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the CNS-derived extracellular vesicles are neuron- derived exosomes (NDEs). 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the GDF15 expression level is mRNA level or protein level. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the GDF15 expression level is significantly higher than the reference. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the GDF15 expression level is at least about 10%, at least about 20%, at least about 30%, at least about 40%, at least about 60%, or at least about 80% higher than the reference. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the ISR modulator is an eIF2B activator. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the ISR modulator is a compound selected from Table 1, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof.
